subject: Follow-Up Email – Heather x Careerspan [Partnership • Landidly • Weston intro]
body:
Hey Heather,

It was great speaking with you last week. Sorry about all the delays!

Discussion Summary: Explored partnership between Careerspan and Landidly, where Heather serves as career coach for their 61 active mid-career job seekers.

Pain Point Uncovered: Landidly struggles with their client tracking tool (Huntr) – potential opportunity for Careerspan to offer better management solution.

Key Opportunities Identified:
* Direct placement pipeline – Careerspan can bypass ATS and place Landidly's candidates directly with employers

Platform Capabilities Demonstrated:
* Vibe Check (job fit assessment)
* Resume tailoring
* Application tracking
* Narrative building features

Action Items:
* Vrijen: Provide Landidly/Heather with a signup link for free access
* Heather: Connect Vrijen with Weston at Landidly for partnership discussions
* Vrijen: Invite Weston to the career tech founder roundtable

I'm actually going to be OOO for the next week and change (finally taking a well-deserved break!) but I'm including @Logan if there's anything urgent.

Else, @Howie can you circle back to this message with a reminder on October 10th for us to reconvene after I'm back from OOO? Just send a response to everyone in this thread. We can chat thereafter! Hopefully, you'll have some good input from your students as well :)

Best,

subject: Follow-Up Email – Hilary x Careerspan [Alumni Support]
body:
Hey Hilary,

I hope things have been going well on your end!

Apologies for the radio silence since the last interaction — interacting with talent leads like yourself gave us a lot of food for thought as far as our strategy is concerned.

As such, I wanted to wait until I had more concrete information about how we're going to operate and the value we can provide. Ultimately, we've opted to focus on primarily sourcing talent for product roles.

We're finalizing partnerships with great communities like Sidebar, Next Play, and Rhize — among others. This is in addition to exclusive partnerships with RPCV Nexus (Peace Corps), Emory University Alumni Association, and others we've locked down

We should start onboarding these communities in the second week of October and seeing a dramatic increase in the number of applicants. Candidly, that's the other reason for holding off — I wanted to be sure that we have enough great candidates to excite the team at Rewiring. Once we get a fresh influx of talent through these upcoming partnerships, I'd love to circle back to see if we can't help you place

You should also know that I passed on your team's extremely patient and eye-opening input on our initial dossier. @Ilse — our Head of Product — is working on refining the report template to better convey

information that adds value beyond what can be gleaned from one's resume.

Lastly, our monetization model remains the same — you only pay for candidates that you want to bring into your process and make it past the initial screening!

I'm away for the next week to handle some personal matters but @Logan will be around in case there's anything we can assist with right away.

Best,

subject: Follow-Up Email – Mark x Vrijen [Intros • Simulations]
body:
Hey Mark—

First, my apologies for the delay in following up—it's been a whirlwind few days, but I wanted to make sure I got you everything I promised.

It was such a pleasure connecting last week, and hearing about your professional and entprereneurial journey. I love how concrete your solution is, and I'd love to stay in touch and find ways to help each other.

Here's what I promised:

* Kamina Singh intro – She's been instrumental in getting us into a dozen+ universities and knows career centers inside-out. She'll be a tremendous ally for your pilots.
* Career/HR tech founder roundtable – You'll love this crew. We have 20+ founders tackling adjacent problems, including several doing B2U go-to-market. Register here for our next session: [link]
* Education@Work contact – They have the same scaling challenge you're solving but from the nonprofit side. Perfect synergy potential. Intro to Stephanie Szuch coming via LinkedIn or email.
* Jeremy Smith (Pega6) – His

thesis aligns beautifully with experiential learning. He's building employer-guaranteed pathways and needs scalable experiences. Fair warning: he's outspoken on LinkedIn, but genuinely mission-driven. Intro incoming.
* Careerspan trial access – Here's a trial link for you and your team to explore: [link]. Tinker around, test it with your use cases, and once you've scoped out what works, let me know how many licenses you'd like for your pilots.
* Future of Higher Education – Register at fohe.org (feel free to use me as your referral). 5,000 members at the intersection of future of work and ed tech—exactly your crowd.

Next steps:
* Watch for my LinkedIn/email intros
* Register for the roundtable when you have a moment
* Let's compare notes after you try the product and have touched base with all these folks

Best,

subject: Follow-Up Email – Hilary x Careerspan [Alumni Support]
body:
Hey Hilary,

Thanks for carving out time on Monday. I loved your point about wanting candidates who

rather than just flooding the pipeline. That's precisely what our pre-processed talent communities deliver – quality over quantity.

As promised, here's everything to support your alumni network:

* Free Careerspan access: [link]
  * Full suite of AI career support free forever + 2 months of tailored job recommendations
  * Ready to share with your LinkedIn alumni group
  * We can distribute directly if that's easier

* Blurb for alumni: [quoted text]

* Workshop offering: Happy to run sessions like

or other topics based on alumni feedback
  * Virtual format, 30-45 minutes
  * We share specific prompts and tactics
  * Led by my cofounder @Logan

For testing Careerspan:
* General testing: Send over any JD and we'll show you the type of candidates we surface and analysis we can provide
* For upcoming roles: We'll run matching analysis, surface candidate profiles without distributing the role, and can include in Monday blasts (just send links by Sunday evening)

I appreciate you mentioning the hesitation around AI bias as well, and wanted to reiterate that our PII scrubbers that flatten data to minimize bias—we're not in the business of those sketchy practices you described from other platforms.

Quick timeline check: You mentioned giving alumni until next Wednesday to respond. I'll stand by for that list and ensure everyone gets immediate access?

Looking forward to showing you how we can save you from that crushing pile of generic applications!

Best,
